function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.

steps.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to estimate the implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts from the SpaceX S-1 filing. I need to:

1. First, parse the SpaceX S-1 filing to get the relevant data
2. Find Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue
3. Find disclosed subscriber counts
4. Calculate implied monthly ARPU
5. State assumptions and identify what the filing's subscriber and revenue definitions exclude

Let me start by parsing the filing.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1_may2026

steps.reasoning_content:
Good, the filing has been parsed. Now I need to retrieve information about:
1. Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue
2. Starlink subscriber counts
3. Definitions of subscribers and revenue

Let me search for these specific pieces of information.

arguments.prompt: What is the Q1 2026 Connectivity segment revenue in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Look for the segment revenue breakdown table or income statement showing Connectivity revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
What are the Starlink subscriber counts disclosed in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Look for subscriber numbers, customer counts, or user counts for Starlink, including any specific dates mentioned.

How does the filing define Starlink subscribers in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? What is included and excluded from the subscriber definition? Look for glossary definitions or footnotes about subscriber counts.

results.content: Based on the provided financial tables and text, the Connectivity segment revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026) was **$3,257 million**.

Based on the provided text, here are the disclosed Starlink subscriber counts, user metrics, and their corresponding dates:

**Quarterly Starlink Subscribers**
*   **March 31, 2026:** 10.3 million subscribers (up ~105% year-over-year)
*   **March 31, 2025:** 5.0 million subscribers 

**Annual Starlink Subscribers (Year-End)**
*   **December 31, 2025:** 8.9 million subscribers
*   **December 31, 2024:** 4.4 million subscribers
*   **December 31, 2023:** 2.3 million subscribers

**Other Related User & Device Metrics (As of March 31, 2026)**
*   **Satellite-to-Mobile Users:** Approximately **7.4 million** monthly unique devices receiving satellite-to-mobile texting and over-the-top voice services across ~30 countries.
*   **Actual Individual End Users:** The text notes that the actual number of individual humans using Starlink is **"meaningfully higher than 10.3 million"** because the company counts unique "Service Lines" (subscriptions), meaning multiple people (e.g., a household or business) can share a single subscriber account.
*   **Potential End Users:** The 164 countries, territories, and markets currently served by Starlink are home to an estimated **3.3 billion** potential end users. 

*(Note: The text explicitly excludes managed enterprise and government customers with direct negotiated contracts—such as aviation, maritime, and government entities—from the "Starlink Subscribers" metric, though it notes enterprise/government revenue and adoption are growing rapidly).*

Based on the provided S-1 preamble and glossary definitions, here is how the filing defines "Starlink Subscribers," including what is counted and what is excluded:

### **Core Definition**
The glossary and operational metrics sections define a **Starlink Subscriber** as a **unique Service Line** that is directly assigned to a Starlink.com account registered to a person or entity that **does not have a direct, negotiated agreement** with the Starlink sales team. 

A **Service Line** is defined as an individual instance of Starlink broadband internet service provisioned under a subscription plan, generally associated with a specific Starlink terminal (or group of terminals), and billed according to Starlink’s standard service plans and terms.

### **What is INCLUDED**
The Starlink Subscribers metric includes standard, non-negotiated subscription plans, specifically:
*   **Personal Plans:** e.g., Residential and Roam.
*   **Business Plans:** e.g., Local Priority and Global Priority.

### **What is EXCLUDED**
The metric explicitly **excludes** managed enterprise and government customers who have direct, negotiated contracts with the Starlink sales team. Excluded domains include:
*   Aviation
*   Maritime
*   Land mobility
*   Fixed sites
*   Government entities

### **Key Nuances & Counting Methodology (Footnotes/Context)**
The filing provides several important clarifications on how subscribers are counted and how the metric relates to actual human users:
*   **Calculation Method:** Starlink Subscribers for a given period are calculated as the total number of unique Service Lines active at the *end* of that period.
*   **Subscribers vs. End Users:** The number of Service Lines is distinct from the number of unique devices, account holders, or physical persons. Because multiple people can share a single Service Line (e.g., a family sharing a single Residential plan in a household), the actual number of individual end-users accessing the network is "meaningfully higher" than the reported subscriber count.
*   **Multiple Service Lines per User:** Conversely, a single individual, household, or business can be counted as *multiple* subscribers if they maintain multiple distinct Service Lines. For example, if a user has both a Residential Service Line and a separate Roam Service Line, they are counted as **two** separate Service Lines, and therefore **two** Starlink Subscribers.

steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have the key data:
- Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue: $3,257 million
- Starlink subscribers as of March 31, 2026: 10.3 million
- Starlink subscribers as of December 31, 2025: 8.9 million

I need to also understand what the Connectivity segment revenue includes - does it include only Starlink subscriber revenue, or does it also include enterprise/government revenue? Let me retrieve more information about the Connectivity segment definition and what revenue it includes.

Also, I need to think about how to calculate ARPU:
- Q1 2026 is 3 months (January, February, March)
- I need average subscribers for the quarter
- Average subscribers = (8.9M + 10.3M) / 2 = 9.6M
- Monthly ARPU = Q1 Connectivity Revenue / (Average Subscribers × 3 months)

But wait - the Connectivity revenue likely includes enterprise/government revenue that is excluded from the subscriber count. I need to understand this better.

Let me retrieve more information about the Connectivity segment revenue composition.

arguments.prompt: What does the Connectivity segment revenue include in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Does it include enterprise, government, aviation, maritime revenue? What are the components of Connectivity segment revenue? Look for segment revenue breakdown and description.
What is the Starlink subscriber count as of December 31, 2025 and March 31, 2026 in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Also look for any average subscriber or average service line calculations for Q1 2026.

What is the definition of the Connectivity segment in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? What revenue streams are included in the Connectivity segment? Does it include hardware/terminal revenue or only service revenue?

results.content:
Based on the provided financial disclosures, **yes**, the Connectivity segment revenue explicitly includes enterprise, government, aviation, and maritime revenues. 

Here is the detailed breakdown and description of the components that make up the Connectivity segment's revenue:

### 1. Breakdown by Customer Segment
The Connectivity segment's revenue is primarily disaggregated into two main reporting categories: **Consumer** and **Enterprise & Government**.

*   **Consumer Revenue:** 
    *   Generated primarily through monthly subscription fees for broadband services (based on geographic market and download speed) and the one-time sale of hardware.
    *   Revenue is recognized ratably over the service period (typically month-to-month).
    *   *Note: Consumer subscribers represented over 60% of Connectivity segment revenue in 2025.*
*   **Enterprise & Government Revenue:**
    *   **Enterprise (Includes Aviation & Maritime):** The text explicitly notes that enterprise revenue growth is driven by the **"aviation, maritime, and other enterprise business,"** as well as mobility. These contracts are structured as a combination of subscriptions, data consumption, and capacity.
    *   **Government (Starshield):** Revenue generated via long-term contracts for **Starshield**, a secure satellite network designed specifically for government customers and national security applications. Revenue for certain multi-year Starshield contracts is recognized over time using a cost-to-cost input method or percentage-of-completion basis.
    *   **Starlink Mobile (MNO Partnerships):** Revenue earned through revenue-sharing arrangements with Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) for satellite-to-mobile services (e.g., SMS, voice, light data). *A specific footnote clarifies that **Starlink Mobile service offerings are included within the Enterprise & Government revenue category**.*

### 2. Breakdown by Offering (Products vs. Services)
The company also disaggregates total company revenue by "Products" and "Services," with the Connectivity segment capturing all product sales:
*   **Products (Hardware):** Revenue from the sale of the **Starlink Kit** (inclusive of the user terminal). Revenue is recorded upon delivery to the customer (or upon installation for certain enterprise customers), net of sales returns and credits. *The text notes that "All of products revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment."*
*   **Services (Connectivity):** Revenue from broadband, mobile connectivity, and Starshield network access. This includes both fixed-price recurring subscriptions and variable-priced services based on actual data consumption.

### Summary of What is Included in Connectivity Revenue:
| Component | Description / Source of Revenue |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Consumer Broadband** | Monthly subscriptions and data usage for residential/consumer users. |
| **Enterprise Broadband** | Contracts for businesses, explicitly including **aviation, maritime, and mobility** sectors. |
| **Government / Starshield** | Long-term, secure national security and government satellite network contracts. |
| **Starlink Mobile** | Revenue-sharing with telecom/MNO partners for direct-to-cell mobile services (categorized under Enterprise & Government). |
| **Starlink Kits (Hardware)** | One-time sales of the satellite terminal and related equipment to all customer classes. |

**In short:** If a customer is paying for internet/data access via the Starlink or Starshield constellations, or buying the physical dish to access it—whether they are a residential user, an airline (aviation), a shipping company (maritime), a telecom carrier (mobile), or a defense agency (government)—that revenue is captured within the **Connectivity Segment**.

Based on the provided text, here is the requested information regarding Starlink's subscriber count and average calculations:

**Starlink Subscriber Count**
*   **December 31, 2025:** 8.9 million
*   **March 31, 2026:** 10.3 million

**Average Subscriber / Average Service Line Calculations for Q1 2026**
The provided text **does not disclose the specific numerical value** for the average number of subscribers or average service lines for Q1 2026. 

However, it does outline the **methodology and formulas** used for these metrics:
*   **End-of-Period Subscribers (Service Lines):** The text defines the reported subscriber count as the end-of-period metric, stating: *"We calculate Starlink Subscribers for a period as the number of unique Service Lines at the end of the period."*
*   **Average Subscribers (used for ARPU):** While the exact average is not provided, the text explains that the average is used as a denominator to calculate ARPU: *"We calculate ARPU as service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers during the period divided by (i) the average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period and by (ii) the number of months in the period."* 

*(Note: The text only provides the resulting ARPU for Q1 2026, which is $66 per month, but does not provide the underlying average subscriber number used to arrive at that figure).*

Based on the provided text, the **Connectivity segment** includes multiple revenue streams and encompasses **both hardware/terminal revenue and service revenue**. 

### Revenue Streams Included in the Connectivity Segment:
1. **Consumer Broadband Services:** Monthly subscription fees based on geographic market and download speed.
2. **Enterprise & Government Services:** Contracts structured around subscriptions, data consumption, capacity, or percentage-of-completion. This includes aviation, maritime, and mobility businesses, as well as **Starshield** (a secure satellite network designed specifically for government and national security applications).
3. **Starlink Mobile Services:** Satellite-to-mobile texting, over-the-top voice, and IoT services, which includes revenue-sharing arrangements and per-user fees with Mobile Network Operator (MNO) partners.
4. **Hardware/Terminal Sales:** The sale of the **Starlink Kit** (inclusive of the terminal) to consumers, enterprises, and government customers.

### Does it include hardware/terminal revenue or only service revenue?
**It includes both.** 
* **Hardware (Products Revenue):** The text explicitly states that customer contracts typically contain multiple performance obligations, which include "(i) the broadband services... and (ii) the sale of the Starlink Kit (inclusive of the terminal)." The company recognizes revenue for the Starlink Kit upon delivery to the customer (or upon installation for certain enterprise customers). Furthermore, the financial disclosures explicitly note that **"All of products revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment."**
* **Services (Service Revenue):** The segment generates service revenue that is recognized over time as the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits (e.g., ratable monthly subscription fees or variable pricing based on actual data consumption). 

In summary, the Connectivity segment's total revenue is a combination of both "Products" (the Starlink hardware kits) and "Services" (the ongoing broadband, mobile, and Starshield connectivity).

steps.reasoning_content:
Excellent! Now I have very important information. The filing actually discloses an ARPU of $66 per month for Q1 2026. Let me also retrieve more details about this ARPU figure and what it includes/excludes.

Key findings so far:
1. Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue: $3,257 million
2. Starlink subscribers: 8.9M (Dec 31, 2025) and 10.3M (Mar 31, 2026)
3. The filing discloses ARPU of $66/month for Q1 2026
4. ARPU is calculated as "service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers during the period divided by (i) the average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period and by (ii) the number of months in the period"

Important: The ARPU calculation uses "service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers" - this means:
- It uses SERVICE revenue only (excludes hardware/products revenue)
- It uses revenue from "Starlink Subscribers" only (excludes enterprise/government revenue since those customers are excluded from the subscriber count)

So the ARPU of $66 is a narrower metric than what I would get by dividing total Connectivity revenue by subscribers.

Let me retrieve more details about the ARPU definition and what it excludes.

arguments.prompt: What is the ARPU disclosed in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? How is ARPU defined? What revenue is included and excluded from the ARPU calculation? What is the ARPU for Q1 2026, Q1 2025, and other periods?
What is the service revenue vs products revenue breakdown for the Connectivity segment in Q1 2026 in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Look for disaggregation of revenue by products and services.
What is the consumer revenue vs enterprise and government revenue breakdown for the Connectivity segment in Q1 2026 and full year 2025 in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}?

results.content:
Based on the provided MD&A and Preamble excerpts, here are the details regarding the company's ARPU (Average Revenue Per User):

### **1. Definition of ARPU**
**Starlink Subscriber ARPU** is defined as the **service revenue** generated from Starlink Subscribers during a specific period divided by:
1. The average number of Starlink Subscribers during that period, and 
2. The number of months in the period.

### **2. Included and Excluded Revenue**
*   **Included:** Only **service revenue** generated from "Starlink Subscribers." This encompasses both **Personal** subscription plans (e.g., Residential and Roam) and **Business** subscription plans (e.g., Local Priority and Global Priority).
*   **Excluded:** 
    *   **Enterprise & Government Revenue:** Revenue from managed enterprise and government customers (including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and government entities) is excluded because these contracts do not fall under the definition of "Starlink Subscribers."
    *   **Product/Hardware Revenue:** Because the metric strictly uses *service* revenue, hardware sales (such as Starlink Kits/user terminals) are excluded from the calculation.

### **3. Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU by Period**
The document discloses the following historical monthly ARPU figures:
*   **Q1 2026** (Three months ended March 31, 2026): **$66**
*   **Q1 2025** (Three months ended March 31, 2025): **$86**
*   **Full Year 2025:** **$81**
*   **Full Year 2024:** **$91**
*   **Full Year 2023:** **$99**

*(Note: The text highlights a 22.9% year-over-year decline in Q1 ARPU, which management attributes to international expansion into lower-income markets and the introduction of lower-priced service plans, prioritizing scale and market access over maintaining high ARPU).*

### **4. Market Assumption ARPU (For TAM Calculations)**
*Separate from the company's actual operational metrics, the text discloses assumed ARPU figures used strictly to calculate the Total Addressable Market (TAM) for future opportunities:*
*   **Residential Broadband TAM:** Assumes a global weighted average of **$31/month** ($43 in high-income, $16 in upper-middle, and $9 in lower-middle/low-income markets).
*   **Starlink Mobile TAM:** Assumes a global weighted average of **$8/month** ($18 in high-income, $5 in upper-middle, and $2 in lower-middle/low-income markets).

Based on the provided financial disclosures, here is the service revenue vs. products revenue breakdown for the Connectivity segment for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026):

* **Products Revenue:** **$380 million** 
*(The revenue recognition note explicitly states that "All of products revenue is attributable to the Connectivity segment.")*
* **Services Revenue:** **$2,877 million** 
*(Calculated by subtracting the $380 million in products revenue from the Connectivity segment's total Q1 2026 revenue of $3,257 million).*

**Total Connectivity Segment Revenue:** **$3,257 million**

Based on the provided financial tables, here is the revenue breakdown for the Connectivity segment (Consumer vs. Enterprise & Government) for Q1 2026 and the full year 2025:

**Q1 2026 (Three Months Ended March 31, 2026)**
* **Consumer:** $2,148 million
* **Enterprise & Government:** $1,109 million
* *(Total Connectivity Revenue: $3,257 million)*

**Full Year 2025 (Year Ended December 31, 2025)**
* **Consumer:** $7,208 million
* **Enterprise & Government:** $4,179 million
* *(Total Connectivity Revenue: $11,387 million)*

*(Note: The financial statements specify that "Enterprise & Government" revenue includes revenue from Starlink Mobile service offerings.)*

steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have all the data I need. Let me compile the analysis:

**Key Data Points:**
1. Q1 2026 Connectivity segment revenue: $3,257 million
   - Consumer: $2,148 million
   - Enterprise & Government: $1,109 million
   - Products (hardware): $380 million
   - Services: $2,877 million

2. Starlink Subscribers:
   - December 31, 2025: 8.9 million
   - March 31, 2026: 10.3 million

3. The filing discloses ARPU of $66/month for Q1 2026

Now let me calculate the implied monthly ARPU myself:

**Method 1: Using total Connectivity revenue (broadest measure)**
- Average subscribers = (8.9M + 10.3M) / 2 = 9.6M
- Monthly ARPU = $3,257M / (9.6M × 3) = $3,257M / 28.8M = $113.09/month
- This is too broad because it includes enterprise/government revenue and hardware revenue

**Method 2: Using Connectivity service revenue only (excludes hardware)**
- Services revenue: $2,877M
- Monthly ARPU = $2,877M / (9.6M × 3) = $2,877M / 28.8M = $99.90/month
- Still too broad because it includes enterprise/government service revenue

**Method 3: Using Consumer revenue only (excludes enterprise/government)**
- Consumer revenue: $2,148M
- Monthly ARPU = $2,148M / (9.6M × 3) = $2,148M / 28.8M = $74.58/month
- This is closer but still includes hardware revenue within consumer

**Method 4: The filing's own ARPU definition**
- Uses "service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers"
- This means: consumer service revenue only (excludes hardware AND enterprise/government)
- Consumer revenue = $2,148M, but this includes both products and services
- We need to figure out how much of the $380M products revenue is consumer vs enterprise

arguments.prompt: What is the Starlink subscriber count as of December 31, 2025, March 31, 2025, and March 31, 2026 in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Are there any quarterly subscriber counts for Q4 2025 or other intermediate periods?
What does the filing say about the decline in ARPU from $86 to $66 in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? What reasons are given for the ARPU decline? Look for management discussion of ARPU trends.

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here are the Starlink subscriber counts for your requested dates:

*   **December 31, 2025:** 8.9 million
*   **March 31, 2025:** 5.0 million
*   **March 31, 2026:** 10.3 million

**Regarding Q4 2025 and other intermediate periods:**
The provided text does not contain subscriber counts for other intermediate periods (such as Q2 or Q3 2025). The data table only reports point-in-time subscriber counts for the end of the first quarter (March 31) and the end of the fiscal year (December 31) for the years presented. 

While the table labels the 8.9 million figure under "Year Ended December 31, 2025," because subscriber counts are a point-in-time metric rather than a cumulative financial flow, this number effectively represents the end-of-Q4 2025 subscriber count. However, no other intermediate quarterly milestones are disclosed in this excerpt.

Based on the provided filing, the decline in Starlink Subscriber ARPU from $86 to $66 (comparing the three months ended March 31, 2025, to the three months ended March 31, 2026) was primarily driven by the following factors:

1. **International Expansion:** The portion of the subscriber base located outside of North America continues to grow. The company uses region-specific pricing to account for varying economic conditions and consumer purchasing power across different countries.
2. **Addition of Lower-Priced Service Plans:** The company has intentionally introduced lower-priced service plans to make its broadband offerings more affordable and accessible across different global markets and economic development contexts.
3. **Fee Adjustments:** The company has made downward adjustments to the monthly service plan fees it charges for its broadband offerings.

**Management's Outlook on ARPU Trends:**
Management explicitly states that they expect Starlink Subscriber ARPU to **continue to decline over the next few years** due to these exact same dynamics (growing international footprint and lower-priced plans). However, they note that this strategic prioritization of scale and market access over high ARPU is expected to be offset by increased operational scale and technological advancements in launch, satellite, and user terminal operations, which will ultimately drive overall revenue growth and cost reduction.

arguments.prompt: What are the reportable segments of SpaceX in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}? Is xAI part of the Connectivity segment or a separate segment? What segment does xAI belong to?

results.content:
Based on the provided document, **xAI is not part of the Connectivity segment; it is a separate segment known as the AI segment.** 

Here are the specific details from the text regarding xAI and its segment classification:

* **Formation of the AI Segment:** The document explicitly states, *"We acquired xAI in February 2026, which forms the basis of our AI segment."*
* **The Three Reportable Segments:** Following the mergers and realignment effective in Q1 2026, the company's Chief Operating Decision Maker (CODM) organizes and measures performance among three distinct operating and reportable segments: **(i) Space, (ii) Connectivity, and (iii) AI.**
* **What the AI Segment Includes:** The AI segment operates a vertically integrated platform that spans the frontier LLM Grok, AI solutions, the X platform (real-time information, entertainment, and free speech), and AI computational infrastructure—all of which are tied to the xAI acquisition and integration. 

Therefore, xAI serves as the foundational pillar for the newly established, standalone **AI segment**.

arguments.final_result:
## Implied Monthly Starlink ARPU Estimate — Q1 2026

### Data Inputs from the S-1 Filing

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue | $3,257 million |
| — Consumer Revenue | $2,148 million |
| — Enterprise & Government Revenue | $1,109 million |
| — Products (Hardware) Revenue | $380 million |
| — Services Revenue (implied) | $2,877 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Dec 31, 2025 | 8.9 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Mar 31, 2026 | 10.3 million |
| Average Subscribers (simple avg.) | 9.6 million |

---

### ARPU Estimates at Different Revenue Perimeters

**1. Total Connectivity Revenue ÷ Average Subscribers (Broadest Measure)**

$$\frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09 \text{/month}$$

This is the simplest calculation but significantly overstates per-subscriber revenue because the numerator includes revenue streams not attributable to the subscriber denominator.

**2. Connectivity Services Revenue Only (Excludes Hardware)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}877\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$99.90 \text{/month}$$

Removes one-time Starlink Kit/terminal sales ($380M) but still includes Enterprise & Government service revenue.

**3. Consumer Revenue Only (Excludes Enterprise & Government)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}148\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$74.58 \text{/month}$$

Removes Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109M) but still includes consumer hardware sales within the numerator.

**4. Filing's Own Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU**

The S-1 discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. This is calculated by the company as:

$$\text{ARPU} = \frac{\text{Service Revenue from Starlink Subscribers}}{\text{Average Starlink Subscribers} \times \text{Months}}$$

Back-solving: $66 × 9.6M × 3 = **~$1,901 million** in service revenue from Starlink Subscribers, implying ~$247M of the $380M hardware revenue is attributable to consumer subscribers and ~$133M to enterprise/government customers.

---

### Key Assumptions

1. **Average subscribers:** I used a simple average of beginning-of-quarter (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-quarter (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026) subscriber counts = 9.6M. The filing does not disclose daily or monthly average subscriber counts. Subscriber growth was rapid (~105% YoY), so the true quarterly average could differ from the simple midpoint if growth was non-linear within the quarter.

2. **Revenue attribution:** The filing does not provide a granular breakdown of how much service revenue is generated specifically from "Starlink Subscribers" (as defined) versus enterprise/government customers. The $66 ARPU figure is the company's own calculation using its internal subscriber-level revenue attribution.

3. **Three-month quarter:** Q1 2026 = January, February, March = 3 months.

4. **Recast financials:** All figures reflect the retrospectively recast consolidated entity (including xAI merged Feb 2, 2026 and X Holdings merged Mar 28, 2025). However, xAI is reported in the separate AI segment, so Connectivity segment figures are unaffected by the xAI merger.

---

### What the Filing's Subscriber and Revenue Definitions Exclude from ARPU

**Excluded from the Subscriber Count (Denominator):**
- **Enterprise & Government customers** with direct negotiated contracts — including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed-site, and government/Starshield entities. These customers generate $1,109M in Q1 2026 revenue but are not counted as "Starlink Subscribers."
- **Starlink Mobile (MNO partnership) end-users** — the ~7.4 million monthly unique devices receiving satellite-to-mobile services are not counted as subscribers; their revenue is classified under Enterprise & Government.
- **Multiple end-users per Service Line** — the filing acknowledges the actual number of individual humans using Starlink is "meaningfully higher" than 10.3M because households and businesses share single Service Lines.

**Excluded from the ARPU Revenue (Numerator):**
- **Hardware/Products revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — the ARPU formula uses only *service* revenue, excluding one-time Starlink Kit terminal sales.
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** — all revenue from negotiated enterprise/government contracts (aviation, maritime, Starshield, MNO partnerships) is excluded from the ARPU numerator because these customers are not "Starlink Subscribers."
- **xAI / AI segment revenue** — reported in a separate segment and not part of Connectivity at all.

**Net Effect:** The filing's $66 ARPU captures only the recurring service revenue from standard consumer and small-business subscription plans (Residential, Roam, Local Priority, Global Priority). The gap between the broadest estimate ($113.09) and the filing's ARPU ($66) is driven primarily by the $1,109M in Enterprise & Government revenue and $380M in hardware revenue that are included in total Connectivity revenue but excluded from the ARPU calculation.

---

### ARPU Trend Context

| Period | Disclosed ARPU |
|---|---|
| FY 2023 | $99 |
| FY 2024 | $91 |
| Q1 2025 | $86 |
| FY 2025 | $81 |
| Q1 2026 | $66 |

The 22.9% YoY decline (Q1 2025 → Q1 2026) is attributed to international expansion into lower-income markets, introduction of lower-priced plans, and fee adjustments. Management expects ARPU to continue declining as the subscriber base shifts toward emerging markets.

---

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (Filed May 20, 2026)"
    }
  ]
}

| — Consumer Revenue | $2,148 million |
| — Enterprise & Government Revenue | $1,109 million |
| — Products (Hardware) Revenue | $380 million |
| — Services Revenue (implied) | $2,877 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Dec 31, 2025 | 8.9 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Mar 31, 2026 | 10.3 million |
| Average Subscribers (simple avg.) | 9.6 million |

---

### ARPU Estimates at Different Revenue Perimeters

**1. Total Connectivity Revenue ÷ Average Subscribers (Broadest Measure)**

$$\frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09 \text{/month}$$

This is the simplest calculation but significantly overstates per-subscriber revenue because the numerator includes revenue streams not attributable to the subscriber denominator.

**2. Connectivity Services Revenue Only (Excludes Hardware)**

Removes one-time Starlink Kit/terminal sales ($380M) but still includes Enterprise & Government service revenue.

**3. Consumer Revenue Only (Excludes Enterprise & Government)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}148\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$74.58 \text{/month}$$

Removes Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109M) but still includes consumer hardware sales within the numerator.

**4. Filing's Own Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU**

The S-1 discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. This is calculated by the company as:

$$\text{ARPU} = \frac{\text{Service Revenue from Starlink Subscribers}}{\text{Average Starlink Subscribers} \times \text{Months}}$$

Back-solving: $66 × 9.6M × 3 = **~$1,901 million** in service revenue from Starlink Subscribers, implying ~$247M of the $380M hardware revenue is attributable to consumer subscribers and ~$133M to enterprise/government customers.

---

### Key Assumptions

1. **Average subscribers:** I used a simple average of beginning-of-quarter (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-quarter (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026) subscriber counts = 9.6M. The filing does not disclose daily or monthly average subscriber counts. Subscriber growth was rapid (~105% YoY), so the true quarterly average could differ from the simple midpoint if growth was non-linear within the quarter.

2. **Revenue attribution:** The filing does not provide a granular breakdown of how much service revenue is generated specifically from "Starlink Subscribers" (as defined) versus enterprise/government customers. The $66 ARPU figure is the company's own calculation using its internal subscriber-level revenue attribution.

3. **Three-month quarter:** Q1 2026 = January, February, March = 3 months.

4. **Recast financials:** All figures reflect the retrospectively recast consolidated entity (including xAI merged Feb 2, 2026 and X Holdings merged Mar 28, 2025). However, xAI is reported in the separate AI segment, so Connectivity segment figures are unaffected by the xAI merger.

---

### What the Filing's Subscriber and Revenue Definitions Exclude from ARPU

**Excluded from the Subscriber Count (Denominator):**
- **Enterprise & Government customers** with direct negotiated contracts — including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed-site, and government/Starshield entities. These customers generate $1,109M in Q1 2026 revenue but are not counted as "Starlink Subscribers."
- **Starlink Mobile (MNO partnership) end-users** — the ~7.4 million monthly unique devices receiving satellite-to-mobile services are not counted as subscribers; their revenue is classified under Enterprise & Government.
- **Multiple end-users per Service Line** — the filing acknowledges the actual number of individual humans using Starlink is "meaningfully higher" than 10.3M because households and businesses share single Service Lines.

**Excluded from the ARPU Revenue (Numerator):**
- **Hardware/Products revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — the ARPU formula uses only *service* revenue, excluding one-time Starlink Kit terminal sales.
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** — all revenue from negotiated enterprise/government contracts (aviation, maritime, Starshield, MNO partnerships) is excluded from the ARPU numerator because these customers are not "Starlink Subscribers."
- **xAI / AI segment revenue** — reported in a separate segment and not part of Connectivity at all.

**Net Effect:** The filing's $66 ARPU captures only the recurring service revenue from standard consumer and small-business subscription plans (Residential, Roam, Local Priority, Global Priority). The gap between the broadest estimate ($113.09) and the filing's ARPU ($66) is driven primarily by the $1,109M in Enterprise & Government revenue and $380M in hardware revenue that are included in total Connectivity revenue but excluded from the ARPU calculation.

---

### ARPU Trend Context

| Period | Disclosed ARPU |
|---|---|

| FY 2025 | $81 |
| Q1 2026 | $66 |
